mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the GDP growth rate signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% decrease in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate and a 1% increase in the consumer price index signal a monetary policy mistake that boosts inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in policy mistake periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the industrial production index signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the retail sales index and a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening consumer sector that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the payroll employment index signals a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
